en.extract:
The green-capped tanager (Stilpnia meyerdeschauenseei) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to forest edge and gardens at altitudes of 1,450–2,200 m (4,760–7,220 ft) in Puno, Peru, and La Paz, Bolivia. It is fairly common and possibly spreading, but its small population has led to it being evaluated as Near Threatened by BirdLife International and IUCN. It closely resembles the widespread burnished-buff tanager (S. cayana), but its mantle is bluer (male) or greener (female), and its crown is greenish-buff. Its specific name commemorates the ornithologist Rodolphe Meyer de Schauensee.

The '''green-capped tanager''' ('''''Stilpnia meyerdeschauenseei''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Thraupidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to forest edge and gardens at altitudes of {{cvt|1450|–|2200|m|-1}} in [[Puno Region|Puno]], [[Peru]], and [[La Paz Department, Bolivia|La Paz]], [[Bolivia]]. It is fairly common and possibly spreading,<ref name=Peru>[[species:Thomas S. Schulenberg|Schulenberg T]], [[species:Douglas F. Stotz|Stotz D]], [[species:Daniel F. Lane|Lane D]], [[species:John Patton O'Neill|O'Neill J]], [[species:Theodore A. Parker III|Parker T III]] (2007). ''Birds of Peru.'' [[Christopher Helm Publishers]]. {{ISBN|978-0-7136-8673-9}}</ref> but its small population has led to it being evaluated as [[Near Threatened]] by [[BirdLife International]] and [[IUCN]].<ref name=IUCN/> It closely resembles the widespread [[burnished-buff tanager]] (''S. cayana''), but its mantle is bluer (male) or greener (female), and its crown is greenish-[[Buff (colour)|buff]].<ref name=Peru/> Its [[specific name (zoology)|specific name]] commemorates the ornithologist [[Rodolphe Meyer de Schauensee]].<ref name="Brewer336">{{cite book |last=Brewer |first=David |date=2018 |title=Birds New to Science: Fifty Years of Avian Discoveries |location=London |publisher=Christopher Helm |page=336 |isbn=978-1-4729-0628-1}}</ref>
